why: Protocol 775 Configuration clientbound minecraft:ping and serverbound minecraft:pong must dispatch from the official framed packet ids to their packet body decoders through Stevenarella packet_by_id. This is packet-support evidence only and is separate from runtime keep-alive echo behavior.
what: The Rust oracle test configuration_ping_pong_framed_dispatch_matches_official_oracle_answer panics while exercising packet::packet_by_id(775, State::Configuration, Direction::Clientbound, packet id 5, official body bytes) because v26_1_2.rs does not map Configuration clientbound packet id 0x05. The test stops before reaching the serverbound pong dispatch check.

evidence.summary: Generated from official client.jar direct calls: ConfigurationProtocols.CLIENTBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ClientboundPingPacket), ConfigurationProtocols.SERVERBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ServerboundPongPacket), both ConfigurationProtocols details().listPackets tables, and ClientboundPingPacket/ServerboundPongPacket getId().
Manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and framed_dispatch_decode comparison surface.
Rust test reads the manifest and answer, splits official framed VarInt packet ids from body bytes, and calls packet::packet_by_id for Configuration clientbound ping and serverbound pong dispatch.

Current Protocol 775 Configuration mapping contains clientbound finish_configuration id 0x03 and keep_alive id 0x04, but not ping id 0x05; serverbound contains finish_configuration id 0x03 and keep_alive id 0x04, but not pong id 0x05.
